metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

ECON-H001.title: claim() Collects ALL Uniswap Position Fees, Stealing Other Users' Shares and Causing Permanent DoS
ECON-H001.duplicate_group_description: claim() calls pool.collect(type(uint128).max) sweeping ALL fees from the shared Uniswap V3 position, stealing other users' uncollected fees (sent to FEE_TAKER) and permanently DoSing subsequent claims via underflow

TECH-H001.title: Whitelist Bypass: Unwhitelisted Addresses Pass checkWhitelist Due to Default Mapping Value
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: Whitelist checkWhitelist() logic flaw: default _whitelistIndex[to]==0 for unwhitelisted addresses bypasses the `_whitelistIndex[to] > _allowedWhitelistIndex` revert when _allowedWhitelistIndex > 0

ECON-H003.title: Per-User Cap Bypass via NFT Transfer Enables Single Actor to Fill Hard Cap
ECON-H003.duplicate_group_description: Per-user cap bypass via NFT transfer: balanceOf(recipient)==0 check allows minting a new position with reset raiseAmount after transferring the existing NFT away

TECH-H004.title: Missing Upper Price Range Validation in initILOPool Allows Invalid Liquidity Position Creation
TECH-H004.duplicate_group_description: Missing validation that initialPoolPriceX96 < sqrtRatioUpperX96 in ILOManager.initILOPool() allows creation of pools with price outside the concentrated liquidity range, breaking all liquidity math

ECON-H008.title: Permissionless initProject Allows Admin Hijacking of Token Pair Projects
ECON-H008.duplicate_group_description: Permissionless initProject() allows anyone to claim project admin for any token pair by calling first, permanently locking out the legitimate project team

ECON-H006.title: buy() Allows Griefing Whitelisted Users by Filling Their Cap Without Consent
ECON-H006.duplicate_group_description: buy(raiseAmount, recipient) allows third-party griefing by filling a whitelisted user's maxCapPerUser allocation without their consent, since payer and recipient are separated

TECH-H007.title: Zero-Duration Vest Schedule Causes Permanent Division-by-Zero DoS on Claims
TECH-H007.duplicate_group_description: Zero-duration vesting schedule (start==end) causes permanent division-by-zero in _unlockedLiquidity via FullMath.mulDiv with zero denominator, permanently DoSing all claims

ECON-H007.title: Oracle TWAP Period Fallback to Near-Spot Price Enables Whitelist Cap Bypass
ECON-H007.duplicate_group_description: TWAP oracle fallback to near-spot price on newly created pools (short observation history) enables price manipulation to bypass Whitelist _maxAddressCap

TECH-H009.title: launch() Sets _launchSucceeded After External Calls Creating Reentrancy Window
TECH-H009.duplicate_group_description: Reentrancy window in ILOPool.launch() where _launchSucceeded is set after external calls (_refundProject transfer and addLiquidity), potentially allowing state machine invariant violation

TECH-H010.title: Liquidity Accounting Mismatch Between Per-User buy() Calculations and Aggregate launch() Calculation
TECH-H010.duplicate_group_description: Liquidity accounting mismatch between per-user floor-division calculations in buy() and aggregate calculation in launch() causes sum of individual liquidities to differ from actual Uniswap position liquidity

TECH-H012.title: ILOPool.buy uses ERC721Enumerable function without inheriting it, potentially bricking buys or enabling inconsistent tokenId selection
TECH-H012.duplicate_group_description: ILOPool.buy() calls tokenOfOwnerByIndex() (ERC721Enumerable function) but contract inherits ERC721 only, potentially bricking repeat purchases or causing inconsistent tokenId resolution

TECH-H014.title: Whitelist.receive() refunds ETH via transfer() causing self-whitelist DoS for contract wallets and potentially blocking whitelist growth
TECH-H014.duplicate_group_description: Whitelist.receive() refunds ETH via transfer() with 2300 gas stipend, causing DoS for contract wallets attempting self-whitelist

TECH-H015.title: Multicall.delegatecall to self may allow bypass of intended per-call invariants when combining state transitions atomically
TECH-H015.duplicate_group_description: Multicall delegatecall-to-self enables atomic state interleaving that may bypass per-call invariant assumptions around lifecycle flags

ECON-H016.title: Whitelist and Contribution Caps Completely Bypassed via Direct Token Transfers
ECON-H016.duplicate_group_description: Whitelist and contribution cap checks only apply to transfers from _pool; direct peer-to-peer or alternative DEX transfers completely bypass all restrictions including blacklist

TECH2-H008.title: ILOPool._unlockedLiquidity() uses unchecked multiplication in Solidity 0.7.6; large totalLiquidity can silently overflow and distort vesting
TECH2-H008.duplicate_group_description: Fee-on-transfer RAISE_TOKEN causes accounting insolvency: buy() records nominal raiseAmount but contract receives less, making last refunders unable to claim full amounts

ECON-H005.title: Uniswap V3 Pool Price Manipulation Griefs Launch Permanently
ECON-H005.duplicate_group_description: ILOManager.launch() requires exact sqrtPriceX96 match with initialPoolPriceX96 on a publicly tradable Uniswap V3 pool, enabling anyone to grief launch indefinitely by moving the price

OPS-H020.title: Early whitelist phase can be fully DoSed if oracle consult period becomes 0 (no observations), reverting all pool buys
OPS-H020.duplicate_group_description: UniswapV3Oracle.peek() with zero observation history causes period=0, making OracleLibrary.consult() revert with 'BP' and DoSing all whitelist-period pool buys

TECH-H013.title: Vultisig.approveAndCall enables arbitrary external call after granting allowance, allowing immediate token drain within callback

TECH-H016.title: ILOManager._initUniV3PoolIfNecessary may allow attacker-chosen initial pool price if pool exists uninitialized (sqrtPriceX96Existing==0)

TECH-H017.title: Initializable.whenNotInitialized lacks revert messages and may mask incorrect initialization flows across clones/manager

OPS-H015.title: Per-user purchase cap can be bypassed by transferring the ILO position NFT to reset balanceOf(recipient)==0 and mint new position

OPS-H016.title: Project initialization is permissionless: attacker can become project admin for a token pair by calling initProject first

OPS-H017.title: Launch can be griefed indefinitely by manipulating Uniswap pool price so ILOManager.launch() fails strict equality check

OPS-H018.title: Vesting schedule can permanently DoS claims if any LinearVest has start==end (division by zero during unlock calc)

OPS-H019.title: Whitelist purchase cap can be bypassed by manipulating the oracle’s effective TWAP window when pool observation history is short

OPS-H021.title: Refund path can become insolvent with fee-on-transfer / deflationary RAISE_TOKEN because accounting uses nominal raiseAmount not actual received

OPS-H022.title: Ownership mis-assignment risk: ILOManager constructor sets owner to tx.origin, enabling unintended privileged actions before initialize()
